notes: MB search sim 1.0 confirms Abbey Lincoln / 1959 / Riverside (RLP 1153). albumDNA is the Abbey Lincoln artist-bio (Anna Marie Wooldridge, jazz vocalist/songwriter/actress, 1960s civil-rights activist) lifted verbatim from the wiki extract — all consistent. wikipediaUrl is null in ours (stored_url field carried the artist page, but ours.wikipediaUrl=null so none_stored). keyTracks Afro Blue/Let Up/Thursday's Child/Lost in the Stars all present in MB tracklist.

Miles Davis / 1959 / Columbia confirmed by MB and wiki (Kind of Blue). Every DNA claim checks against wiki: 30th Street Studio, Mar 2 & Apr 22 1959, Coltrane & Cannonball, Bill Evans with Wynton Kelly on Freddie Freeloader, Paul Chambers, Jimmy Cobb, George Russell influence, 5x Platinum / five million copies. MB tracklist only lists So What/Blue in Green/All Blues but wiki recording section confirms Freddie Freeloader and Flamenco Sketches — all five keyTracks match.

MB embedded_mbid confirms artists Louis Armstrong + The Dukes of Dixieland, releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 1960, title match; all five keyTracks (Bourbon Street Parade/South/Washington and Lee Swing/Avalon/New Orleans) and the three DNA-named tunes present in MB tracklist. The label field 'Audio Fidelity' cannot be confirmed: MB resolved to a hi-res reissue on 'High Definition Tape Transfers' (HDTT2584), which does not surface the original 1960 issuing label — not a contradiction, just uncheckable. ours.wikipediaUrl is null; the stored_url that was searched points to a different Armstrong album (I've Got the World on a String) but ours ships no URL.

Catalogued artist is John Coltrane but he is a credited sideman; the DNA correctly states the album is 'led by jazz pianist Red Garland' with Coltrane on tenor and Donald Byrd on trumpet. MB (artists The Red Garland Quintet, John Coltrane, Donald Byrd; Prestige P-7181; releaseGroup 1960) and wiki both confirm: Nov 15 1957 session, same lineup as All Mornin' Long, Woody'n You, Birks' Works, I've Got It Bad (Ellington). All five keyTracks match MB tracklist.

MB embedded_mbid confirms Bobby Timmons / Riverside (RS 9334) / date 1960-11, matching DNA's 'released on Riverside in November 1960.' All five keyTracks (Soul Time/So Tired/The Touch of Your Lips/S'posin'/Stella B.) present in MB tracklist. The originals-vs-standards attribution (title track and Stella B. as originals; Touch of Your Lips and S'posin' as standards) is not in MB but is uncontradicted and uncontroversial. ours.wikipediaUrl null; the searched stored_url was a different Timmons album (Holiday Soul, 1964) but ours ships no URL.

MB search sim 1.0 confirms Yusef Lateef / 1960 / Riverside (RLP 337). All four keyTracks (The Centaur and the Phoenix/Summer Song/Revelation/Apathy) present in MB tracklist. DNA is overwhelmingly editorial (pioneer of non-Western instruments, world-jazz-fusion-before-the-term) which is opinion, not a flag. The only concrete factual aside — Lateef playing oboe/flute/tenor sax on the album — is not in the evidence but is uncontradicted; core identity confirmed, so clean.

Don Ellis / Candid (CJS 9004) / 1961 confirmed by MB embedded_mbid; releaseGroup title '... How Time Passes...' matches ours. Wiki (How Time Passes) confirms debut album, recorded 1960, released Candid. DNA matches wiki verbatim. All five keyTracks match MB tracklist exactly.

MB search sim 1.0 confirms Baby Face Willette / Blue Note (BLP 4068) / 1961-07. Wiki (Face to Face, Baby Face Willette album) confirms debut, Blue Note 1961, personnel Grant Green (guitar), Fred Jackson (tenor sax). DNA's specific claims — debut, Fred Jackson tenor, Grant Green guitar — all verified by wiki personnel. All four keyTracks match MB tracklist.

MB embedded_mbid confirms artists Pim Jacobs, Rita Reys, Kenny Clarke; Philips; releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 1961. DNA's '1961 date for Philips credited to Pim Jacobs, Rita Reys and Kenny Clarke' matches the MB artist credit exactly, and the two named tunes plus all five keyTracks (I'm Gonna Sit Right Down.../Autumn Leaves/Cherokee/Poor Butterfly/Can't We Be Friends) are in the MB tracklist. No wiki page resolved.

Phil Woods / Candid (CJS 9016) / 1961 confirmed by MB embedded_mbid and wiki (Rights of Swing). DNA's first sentence matches wiki verbatim; the rest is editorial. All five keyTracks (Prelude and Part I through Part V (Presto)) match MB tracklist exactly.

Catalogued artist Eric Dolphy is a sideman; DNA correctly states the album is 'led by saxophonist Oliver Nelson... with Eric Dolphy as a sideman on bass clarinet and alto' and a 'sextet date' — all matching MB (Oliver Nelson Sextet, Eric Dolphy, Richard Williams) and wiki personnel (Dolphy: bass clarinet, alto). All five keyTracks match MB tracklist. Label note: ours field says 'Prestige'; DNA and wiki say the original 1961 issue was on New Jazz Records (a Prestige imprint), and the MB reissue is on Original Jazz Classics — same corporate family, so 'Prestige' is not a clear contradiction. Year 1961 confirmed (MB releaseGroup 1961-01, wiki '1961').

Shirley Scott Plays Horace Silver / Prestige (7240) / 1961 confirmed by MB search sim 1.0 and wiki. DNA matches wiki (organist Shirley Scott, Horace Silver compositions, recorded 1961, Prestige). All four keyTracks match MB tracklist (Moon Ray = MB 'Moon Ray' / wiki 'Moonray', same track). Wiki shortDescription says '1962 studio album' but its body says 'recorded in 1961 and released on Prestige'; ours year 1961 aligns with the body and MB date 1961.

Abbey Lincoln / Candid (CJM 8015) / 1961 confirmed by MB search sim 1.0 and wiki (Straight Ahead, Abbey Lincoln album). DNA's first sentence matches wiki verbatim; the We Insist!/political-weight framing is editorial context. All four keyTracks (Blue Monk/When Malindy Sings/In the Red/Straight Ahead) match MB tracklist.

Together! / Atlantic (1428) / 1961, drummers Elvin Jones and Philly Joe Jones, confirmed by MB embedded_mbid and wiki. DNA's album personnel (Blue Mitchell, Curtis Fuller, Hank Mobley, Wynton Kelly, Paul Chambers) match wiki personnel exactly; first sentence verbatim from wiki. All three keyTracks (Le Roi/Beau-Ty/Brown Sugar) match MB tracklist. The appended bio aside that Elvin's brothers Hank and Thad were also jazz musicians is not in this evidence (uncheckable) but is tangential to an otherwise fully verified album; not flagged as a contradiction.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Waltz for Debby (1962 album), album by Bill Evans

wrongClaims.ourClaim: Waltz for Debby is a 1961 live album credited to the Bill Evans Trio
wrongClaims.problem: Release year is 1962, not 1961; ours states (and the year field is) 1961. Recorded live at the Village Vanguard in June 1961 but issued in 1962.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: Waltz for Debby (1962 album), album by Bill Evans

notes: Right entity (Bill Evans Trio / Riverside RLP-9399 / live, all confirmed by MB; catalog number and Live secondaryType match). Year is the sole error: ours=1961 but MB release date=1962, MB releaseGroup firstReleaseDate=1962, and the wiki disambiguation page explicitly lists 'Waltz for Debby (1962 album), album by Bill Evans.' Three converging signals -> factual_error on year. (The 1961 recording date is real, which likely caused the slip.) All four keyTracks match MB tracklist. ours.wikipediaUrl is null (the searched stored_url is a disambiguation page, not an album page), so none_stored.

MB embedded_mbid confirms title / artists Nancy Wilson + Cannonball Adderley / Capitol Records (ST 1657) / date 1962-09 — matches ours exactly; all five keyTracks present in MB tracklist. The stored_url that was searched is the WRONG entity (Somethin' Else, 1958 Adderley/Blue Note), so it provides no personnel and ours ships no URL anyway. Several substantive DNA claims cannot be checked against surviving evidence: Nat Adderley on cornet and Joe Zawinul on piano (no personnel in MB; wiki is wrong entity), the direct quote 'a sort of easy-going third horn,' and the pop-chart claim. No contradictions, but these uncheckable specifics drive an unverifiable verdict.

MB embedded_mbid confirms Baby Face Willette / Blue Note / releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 1962 (MB release itself is a 1999 JP reissue, TOCJ-9143). DNA's claims — Blue Note, 1962, tracks Willow Weep for Me / Stop and Listen / At Last — all confirmed; all five keyTracks present in MB tracklist. No wiki page resolved; ours ships no URL.

Herbie Hancock / Blue Note (BLP 4109) / 1962 confirmed by MB search sim 1.0 and wiki (Takin' Off). DNA matches wiki near-verbatim: debut, Dexter Gordon, Freddie Hubbard, Butch Warren, Billy Higgins, hard bop, Watermelon Man near Top 100 / standard, Head Hunters 1973. All four keyTracks match MB tracklist.

MB returned no release and wiki opensearch found nothing, but websearch resolved=true with Wikipedia + Discogs sources: 'The Newest Sound Around is an album by singer Jeanne Lee and pianist Ran Blake,' debut for both, 1962, RCA Victor (LSP-2500). Year and label match ours; wikipediaUrl now correctly stored. All four keyTracks (Laura/Where Flamingos Fly/Evil Blues/Church on Russell Street) appear in the websearch tracklist. DNA is editorial (noir miniatures, third stream, Monk-influenced piano) and uncontradicted. keyTracksVerdict ok against websearch tracklist.

Jack Teagarden / Verve Records, Inc. (V-8465) / 1962 confirmed by MB embedded_mbid and wiki. DNA matches the wiki lead verbatim (Willard Robison songs, orchestra arranged/conducted by Bob Brookmeyer or Russ Case, Verve). All six keyTracks match MB tracklist exactly.

MB embedded_mbid confirms Sonny Rollins / RCA Victor (LSP-2572) / 1962 — matches ours including the catalogue number cited in the DNA. All five keyTracks (If Ever I Would Leave You/Jungoso/Bluesongo/The Night Has a Thousand Eyes/Brownskin Girl) match MB tracklist exactly, and the three DNA-named tracks are among them. The stored_url that was searched is the WRONG entity (The Bridge, 1962 Rollins/RCA) but ours.wikipediaUrl is null. Core identity and tracks fully verified by MB -> clean.

Right entity: MB search sim 1.0 confirms Art Blakey & The Jazz Messengers / Riverside (RM 438) / Caravan; all four keyTracks and all five DNA-named tunes present in MB tracklist. Year discrepancy: ours=1963 but the only date evidence (MB release date and releaseGroup firstReleaseDate) is 1962. This is a known recording-late-1962 / issue-boundary case where catalog sources legitimately split between 1962 and 1963, so confidence is moderate — a human reviewer may keep 1963. Flagged as factual_error per the evidence (MB=1962) since I must judge ours-vs-evidence only. The searched stored_url is the WRONG entity (Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers, 1961 Impulse! album); ours ships no URL.

Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins / impulse! (AS 26) / Feb 1963 confirmed by MB embedded_mbid and wiki. DNA matches wiki lead verbatim (recorded Aug 18 1962, released Feb 1963, Impulse!); 'octet' matches the eight-musician wiki personnel. All five keyTracks match MB tracklist.

Same album as the Ellington-credited entry, here credited to co-artist Coleman Hawkins (a credited artist on the MB release). MB embedded_mbid and wiki confirm impulse! (AS 26) / Feb 1963 / recorded Aug 18 1962. DNA's first sentence matches wiki verbatim; 'octet' matches eight-musician personnel; the rest is editorial. All five keyTracks match MB tracklist.

No evidence document survived entity-matching. MB returned no release (searchCandidates empty). The searched stored_url is the WRONG entity entirely: Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes, an American soul/R&B vocal group, not the South African jazz collective The Blue Notes (Chris McGregor's group) implied by the keyTracks (Kippie, Poppie, Township Bop, Ke Tla Moya) and the Gallo label. ours.wikipediaUrl is null. No websearch provided. Title 'Blue Notes in Concert', year 1964, label Gallo, and all keyTracks are unverifiable against any surviving source -> no_evidence.
